title: ctLab: add Segor article notes
description: Goal: Add Obsidian note callouts with matching Segor article links to local ctLab articles, including the Segor overview link in Fernwirkung. Definition of Done: Matching ctLab articles contain > [!note] callouts with the relevant Segor page links, changes are verified, committed, and pushed. Notes: Use the current local ctLab file set and avoid unrelated workspace changes.
close_reason: Closed

title: ctLab: audit and repair external links
description:
Goal:
Audit external links in ctLab articles, remove dead links, and replace them when a current reachable source can be found.
Definition of Done:
Each affected ctLab article has been checked, unreachable links have been removed or updated, and changes have been pushed.
Notes:
User requested one sub-agent per article.
close_reason: Closed

title: ctLab: normalize Stückliste tables
description:
Goal:
Normalize Stückliste formatting in ctLab markdown articles to match ctLab/audio-pedant-291478.md.
Definition of Done:
All ctLab articles with a Stückliste use the same markdown table structure as the reference article and the changes are validated by inspection.
Notes:
Reference file: ctLab/audio-pedant-291478.md.
close_reason: Closed

title: ctLab: port Pascal comments into Rust files
description:
Goal:
Carry the original Pascal comment context into the Rust firmware ports so the Rust sources retain the original intent and version-history notes.
Definition of Done:
The Rust files under ctLab/Firmware-rust include ported Pascal header comments in a readable Rust comment form and the project still passes cargo check/test.
Notes:
Prefer preserving original wording where practical, but normalize encoding for readability.
close_reason: Closed

title: ctLab: port firmware Pascal to Rust
description: Goal: Port the c't-Lab Pascal firmware sources under ctLab/Firmware to Rust code in a parallel Rust tree without committing. Definition of Done: A Rust port exists for each Pascal source file, the output is organized consistently, and remaining gaps are documented. Notes: Best-effort source port; do not commit changes.
close_reason: Closed

title: ctLab: migrate firmware Rust port to avrd
description:
Goal:
Replace the mock hardware layers in ctLab/Firmware-rust with avrd-based register access that stays close to the original Pascal firmware.
Definition of Done:
There is a Cargo-based Rust scaffold under ctLab/Firmware-rust using avrd, the hardware modules are migrated to avrd-backed code, and the tree builds or type-checks with documented limitations.
Notes:
Preserve the existing source-port structure and only abstract where necessary.
close_reason: Closed

title: ctLab: import Heise archive articles
description:
Goal:
Download the c't Lab archive article texts and images linked from the provided Heise overview page into ./.temp/ and create markdown versions under ./ctLab/.
Definition of Done:
All discovered archive article pages from the provided link are saved locally, their images are downloaded into ./.temp/, markdown article files are created in ./ctLab/, and beads state is synced.
Notes:
Preserve source URLs in the generated markdown and avoid overwriting unrelated user changes.
close_reason: Closed

title: attachments: rotate stromabnehmer image
description: Goal: Rotate Attachements/stromabnehmer-291588-04.jpeg by 90 degrees clockwise. Definition of Done: The image file is rotated in place, verified, committed, and pushed. Notes: Requested directly by the user.
close_reason: Closed

title: ctLab: download firmware sources
description: Goal: Download the available c't-Lab firmware source files into ctLab/Firmware, with emphasis on clarifying the IFP case. Definition of Done: Source-bearing firmware files from the surviving mirror are stored under ctLab/Firmware, a short manifest documents what was downloaded and what has no separate firmware, and the result is committed and pushed. Notes: Do not disturb unrelated local ctLab or .obsidian changes.
close_reason: Closed

title: ctLab: audit Pascal-to-Rust port gaps
description:
Goal:
Audit every Pascal/Rust firmware file pair for missing or incomplete behavior and turn each gap into a child bead under the port-fidelity parent.
Definition of Done:
All file pairs are compared, every concrete gap is captured as a child issue with clear references, and the audit issue can be closed.
Notes:
This is an analysis/tracking task, not an implementation task.
close_reason: Audit completed and all identified gaps were filed as child tickets.

title: ctLab: complete firmware Pascal-to-Rust port fidelity
description:
Goal:
Track the remaining semantic gaps between the original ctLab Pascal firmware sources and the Rust ports under ctLab/Firmware-rust.
Definition of Done:
Every identified lost behavior, undocumented semantic constant, missing initialization path, parser omission, hardware-control omission, and no-op placeholder has a tracked child issue and the parent reflects the overall port-fidelity work.
Notes:
This issue is the parent for per-gap tasks discovered by file-pair audits.
close_reason: All tracked ctLab Pascal-to-Rust firmware parity child tickets are closed; final cargo test/check and Pascal-file guard pass.

title: ctLab: restore ACV command table parity
description:
Goal:
Restore the missing Pascal behavior for `ctLab: restore ACV command table parity`.
Definition of Done:
- The Rust port matches the relevant Pascal behavior for this gap.
- Any intentional deviation is explicitly documented and covered by tests where practical.
Notes:
Compare ctLab/Firmware/ACV/ACV.pas with ctLab/Firmware-rust/ACV/ACV.rs. Gap: named-command dispatch no longer preserves the Pascal command table and CmdWhich ordering breaks CMD2_SUB_CH_ARR indexing.
close_reason: Rust ACV command table parity restored in shared workspace and verified against ACV.pas; cargo tests pass.

description:
